paper-gray.$description: Paper Gray — Page background, card borders, hairline dividers — the cool, slightly blue-gray substrate that every surface sits on or against

bone-white.$description: Bone White — Card and content-tile surface — reads as warm white against the cool Paper Gray substrate, giving each block a subtle lift without any shadow

full-black.$description: Full Black — Primary body text, nav logo, image letterbox bars — maximum contrast, used for sentences that need to carry the page

ink-charcoal.$description: Ink Charcoal — Headline and link text — almost black but not quite, so Fraunces serifs feel printed rather than digital

soft-graphite.$description: Soft Graphite — Secondary body text, supporting metadata lines under headings

pencil-gray.$description: Pencil Gray — Tertiary text, icon strokes, placeholder labels — fades to the background and only earns attention when scanned for

highlight-mint.$description: Highlight Mint — Pill-tag background (FILTER, READ) — the first chromatic accent tier, like a fluorescent marker on the page

highlight-butter.$description: Highlight Butter — Pill-tag background (NOW BREWING) — second accent tier, warmer counterpart to the mint

highlight-sage.$description: Highlight Sage — Pill-tag background (READ variants) — third accent tier, softer green for less emphatic tags

rust-underline.$description: Rust Underline — Occasional inline emphasis — the only warm chromatic voice in the palette, used sparingly to single out one word or phrase inside a body sentence

navy-quotation.$description: Navy Quotation — Occasional inline emphasis — the cool counterpart to Rust Underline, used for the same hand-underline-in-a-book purpose

fraunces.$description: Display and editorial headings — light-weight serif at 30/36/60px. The whisper weight and optical-size personality of Fraunces make every headline feel hand-set rather than UI-generated; this is the single most distinctive typographic choice on the site

inter.$description: Body, navigation, metadata, card titles, footer. Used everywhere the voice is functional and not editorial. Compact 14px for card-level metadata, comfortable 16px for running prose

ui-monospace.$description: Code-fence and small technical labels — inherits the same tight tracking as Inter so mono blocks align with the proportional text around them

drafting-paper.$description: Surface level 0: Page substrate — everything sits on or is defined against this color

bone-white-card.$description: Surface level 1: Standard content card or project tile

highlight-pill.$description: Surface level 2: Accent tag surface floating on a card
